What pass requires: Every factual statement (dates; names; firsts; numbers; quotes; locations; events) was listed and shown to the author before any evaluation
Notes: The author sees what is being checked

What pass requires: Each claim is marked confirmed / contradicted / unverifiable — no claim skipped
Every confirmed or contradicted verdict cites a specific source (URL; book; archive) — never "multiple sources"
The check ends with exactly one of: ✅ all verified / ⚠️ N need attention / ❌ N incorrect; with the flagged claims listed
Notes: One glance tells the author where the draft stands

What pass requires: The ✅ verdict was only given when every single claim was verified against a source
Notes: Never clear a draft with unverified claims outstanding

What pass requires: Unverifiable claims come with the author's options (cut / soften / find a primary source) — the assistant did not decide or rewrite
If live sources were unavailable; the check was framed as a consistency check; nothing got ✅; and the best overall verdict given was ⚠️
Notes: Applies only when web access was unavailable — skip otherwise
